steps.title: Identify the structural contrast
steps.content: The passage describes shared aesthetic features (moonlit waters, mangrove trees, compositional structures) but then pivots with “But there was room for individuation:”, showing how individual artists introduced variations despite these shared conventions.

steps.title: Match the structure to the answer choices
steps.content:
Choice A — It describes an aesthetic framework shared by a particular group of artists and then makes and illustrates the claim that individuals introduced variations within that framework. identifies both parts of the passage: the shared aesthetic framework and the claim that individuals introduced variations within it, illustrated by Al Black's distinctive style.

The other choices either introduce concepts not in the passage (B's derivative perception, C's collaboration and influence) or misstate the scope (D extends the variation to multiple members rather than one example).

That matches Choice A — It describes an aesthetic framework shared by a particular group of artists and then makes and illustrates the claim that individuals introduced variations within that framework..
